prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ARIMA', seasonality_champion='CWT_svr', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.8971) and high seasonality variance (0.7059), with ARIMA best tracking trends and CWT_svr capturing seasonality. Early horizon specialists (catboost, svr) differ significantly from late horizon specialists (ONLY_FT_svr, ARIMA), indicating strong per-horizon specialization. Given the combination of high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is strongly recommended to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating individual weaknesses.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ARIMA",
  "seasonality_champion": "CWT_svr",
  "overall_champion": "FT_svr",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "catboost", "late": "ONLY_FT_svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["FT_svr", "rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "FT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["CWT_rf", "NaiveMovingAverage", "DWT_catboost", "THETA", "ONLY_CWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.8971,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.8971) and high seasonality variance (0.7059), with ARIMA best tracking trends and CWT_svr capturing seasonality. Early horizon specialists (catboost, svr) differ significantly from late horizon specialists (ONLY_FT_svr, ARIMA), indicating strong per-horizon specialization. Given the combination of high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is strongly recommended to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating individual weaknesses."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k4"],
  "params_overrides": {"inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35": {"shrinkage": 0.35, "top_k": 5}, "topk_mean_per_horizon_k4": {"top_k": 4}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong per-horizon specialization, favoring best_per_horizon_by_validation and topk_mean_per_horizon_k4. relative_spread_mean=0.495 (high disagreement) justifies dba_combination for phase-shift robustness. RMSE spread ratio=0.8971 between FT_svr (1878.42) and catboost (3331.97) warrants inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 to leverage model dispersion. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.7059). Using rmse_focus preset aligns with high model dispersion flag."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity (2 selection: best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k4; 1 weighted: inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35; 1 baseline: dba_combination). No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. All 4 candidates are in the top ranking (scores 0.918-0.984). Universe leaderboards show better individual metric performance for some candidates, but rules only require adding when we have 0 of a type (we have both selection and weighted). No redundancy issues (no ≥4 same-type candidates). n_windows=3 satisfies preference for weighted candidates which we already have.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["poly_weighted_average_p0.5_trim0.8"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "High disagreement (relative_spread_mean=0.495 >= 0.25) and low n_windows=3 favor robust weighted methods. Universe leaderboard shows poly_weighted_average_p0.5_trim0.8 has best RMSE (1720.05) vs current best (1838.68). Adding this improves candidate set diversity with strong performance.", "changes": ["added poly_weighted_average_p0.5_trim0.8 for high disagreement and low n_windows"], "when_good": "When relative_spread_mean < 0.25 and n_windows >= 6"}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.8971) and high seasonality variance (0.7059), with ARIMA best tracking trends and CWT_svr capturing seasonality. Early horizon specialists (catboost, svr) differ significantly from late horizon specialists (ONLY_FT_svr, ARIMA), indicating strong per-horizon specialization. Given the combination of high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is strongly recommended to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating individual weaknesses.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong per-horizon specialization, favoring best_per_horizon_by_validation and topk_mean_per_horizon_k4. relative_spread_mean=0.495 (high disagreement) justifies dba_combination for phase-shift robustness. RMSE spread ratio=0.8971 between FT_svr (1878.42) and catboost (3331.97) warrants inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 to leverage model dispersion. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.7059). Using rmse_focus preset aligns with high model dispersion flag.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity (2 selection: best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k4; 1 weighted: inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35; 1 baseline: dba_combination). No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. All 4 candidates are in the top ranking (scores 0.918-0.984). Universe leaderboards show better individual metric performance for some candidates, but rules only require adding when we have 0 of a type (we have both selection and weighted). No redundancy issues (no ≥4 same-type candidates). n_windows=3 satisfies preference for weighted candidates which we already have.

statistician.rationale: High disagreement (relative_spread_mean=0.495 >= 0.25) and low n_windows=3 favor robust weighted methods. Universe leaderboard shows poly_weighted_average_p0.5_trim0.8 has best RMSE (1720.05) vs current best (1838.68). Adding this improves candidate set diversity with strong performance.
changes: added poly_weighted_average_p0.5_trim0.8 for high disagreement and low n_windows
